Technical picture provides confirmation of active distribution: below SMA50, MACD bearish, near lower BB, ADX=30 showing established downtrend momentum. Exit per Lesson 9 (trendless + distributing) and Lesson 34 (guidance cut as leading indicator of multi-quarter derating cycle).

lessons: Lesson 34: When a retail or consumer-facing portfolio company issues a full-year EPS guidance cut below consensus, treat this as thesis invalidation and exit immediately — do not wait for further technical deterioration or the catastrophic stop. A guidance cut in consumer discretionary or discount retail signals the macro tailwinds are not flowing through as modeled, and guidance cuts in this sector tend to cluster: the first cut precedes further cuts as the underlying revenue shortfall compounds. The tell that distinguishes a thesis-level guidance cut from noise is: (1) full-year guidance reduced (not just a quarterly miss), AND (2) at least two technical distribution signals present (below SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV falling/flat). Trigger condition: full-year guidance below consensus + two technical distribution signals → exit immediately regardless of whether the position is at a gain or loss. Do not hold hoping for a recovery — the guidance cut is the market telling you the fundamental driver of the thesis is weaker than modeled.

new_positions.thesis: NVIDIA is the foundational infrastructure of the AI revolution and the undisputed market leader in GPU-accelerated computing. Every major AI training workload — from GPT models to autonomous vehicles to protein folding — runs on NVIDIA's CUDA ecosystem. The software moat (CUDA is irreplaceable in production AI pipelines) is as durable as any in technology. The semiconductor supercycle thesis remains intact: AI/ML workloads are doubling GPU demand while supply chains are constrained. Recent technical weakness (RSI=36, below SMA50) appears to be a profit-taking rotation, not fundamental deterioration — the critical tell is OBV RISING despite the price decline, confirming institutions are accumulating into the weakness rather than distributing. MACD turning bullish signals momentum reversal is underway. ADX=51 at this RSI level, combined with rising OBV, indicates we are buying into the final leg of the pullback as institutions load up. This is the highest-conviction single stock expression of our AI/Automation theme entering at a meaningful discount to recent highs. Lesson 29 explicitly does NOT apply here — that lesson requires OBV FALLING; here OBV is rising, which is the opposite accumulation signal.

new_positions.thesis: BlackRock is the dominant global asset manager with $9T+ AUM and the most direct intersection of our Finance and Climate Transition themes. Larry Fink's ESG mandate is now federal policy: SEC climate risk disclosure requirements and Biden Treasury ESG investment guidelines are structurally directing institutional capital flows through BlackRock's iShares ESG franchise — the fastest-growing segment of the world's largest AUM platform. Every dollar entering equity markets increases BlackRock's fee revenue through passive vehicles. The fiscal stimulus environment (more capital in the system) and rising equity markets create a structural AUM tailwind. Unlike trading-heavy financials (GS, JPM), BlackRock's fee-based model provides earnings stability uncorrelated with market volatility events. RSI=59 is a clean non-overbought entry, MACD bullish confirms momentum resumption, OBV rising confirms institutional accumulation, ADX=21 shows early trend formation rather than exhaustion. This fills the Finance theme gap created by closing LLY while adding Climate Transition theme exposure via the ESG reallocation mega-trend.
new_positions.invalidation: SEC reverses course on ESG disclosure requirements (structural thesis invalidation). Alternatively, AUM growth decelerates significantly in two consecutive quarters signaling the ESG inflow thesis is not materializing. Technical: MACD bearish + OBV falling simultaneously (Lesson 22 pre-conditions activating).

lessons: Lesson 35: When a government public health authority (FDA, CDC, Fauci/White House) makes public statements that reframe the standard of care in a direction that reduces a portfolio company's competitive differentiation (e.g., 3-dose booster becoming standard vs. JNJ's single-dose), treat this as a near-term catalyst erosion event — not a long-term thesis destruction. The trigger is: (1) named public health official publicly signals a shift in vaccination standard that disadvantages a specific vaccine platform, AND (2) corroborating market evidence appears within the same week (e.g., European governments returning vaccine inventory). Combined with technical distribution signals (MACD bearish + OBV falling), exit the position. The key distinction from full thesis invalidation: this is a near-term competitive repositioning, not a permanent defeat — but the near-term re-rating is negative and capital compounds better elsewhere while the narrative settles.

new_positions.thesis: Taiwan Semiconductor is the foundational chokepoint of the global AI and chip supercycle. NVDA's position in our portfolio is recovering (MACD bullish + OBV rising), validating that the semiconductor demand thesis is intact. TSM manufactures every critical AI chip — NVIDIA GPUs, AMD EPYC, Apple Silicon — and operates 2-3 years ahead of any credible competitor in process node leadership. The Apple 'California Streaming' event this week is a near-term catalyst for Apple Silicon demand, which flows directly through TSMC's advanced nodes. Semiconductor shortage conditions remain acute across automotive, consumer electronics, and 5G, providing pricing power and a backlog that is 12-24 months deep. Re-entering a prior closed thesis at $123.62 vs prior entry of $118.69 — essentially same price with materially better technical setup: MACD bullish, RSI=65 (healthy), above SMA50, OBV rising, ADX=24 (trend formation). This is AI/Automation theme infrastructure exposure with no binary event risk.

lessons: Lesson 36: When a strong downtrend position (high ADX, below SMA50) simultaneously shows MACD turning bullish AND OBV rising, treat this as an early institutional accumulation signal within the downtrend — NOT a falling knife per Lesson 29. lessons: Lesson 37: When a market-wide correction produces MACD bearish readings across virtually every sector simultaneously — without a single identifiable systemic event (unlike Lesson 7's GameStop/Archegos triggers) — the correct response is NOT to rotate aggressively but to use OBV direction as the primary triage tool: (1) positions with OBV rising during the broad selloff are experiencing institutional accumulation and should be held with conviction — the institutions are buying the dip in these names specifically; (2) positions with OBV falling during the broad selloff are experiencing institutional distribution that happens to coincide with the correction — these are the highest exit priorities; (3) positions with OBV flat are neutral — hold while monitoring for directional commitment. In a market-wide correction, the divergence between OBV rising vs. OBV falling names is more informative than absolute price action, because it separates smart money accumulation from distribution disguised as 'the market going down.' Trigger condition: when 8+ positions simultaneously show MACD bearish with no single known systemic catalyst → sort positions by OBV direction and prioritize exits among OBV falling names with existing Lesson 22/19 gain or loss conditions.

lessons: Lesson 39: When a portfolio company files a mixed shelf offering (S-3/mixed shelf registration), treat it as a near-term dilution overhang that suppresses price for weeks to quarters — regardless of whether the offering is immediately executed. Management files a shelf when they believe equity is fairly-to-overvalued and want optionality to sell shares; this is fundamentally incompatible with the bull thesis and particularly damaging to loss positions near stops. The dilution overhang typically prevents technical recovery as the market prices in potential supply at current levels. Trigger condition: mixed shelf or S-3 registration filed AND position is currently at a loss AND price is within 10% of the stop level → exit immediately without waiting for stop execution. A small additional loss from early exit is always preferable to a stop execution compounded by shelf-driven suppression. Key distinction: shelf filings by high-conviction fundamental winners (NVDA, TSM) with OBV rising do not trigger this — the rule applies specifically to loss positions near stops where the dilution risk is asymmetrically negative.

lessons: Lesson 40: When a corporate whistleblower provides documented internal research to government regulators across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously (US Congress + EU + UK Parliament in the same week), treat it as a structural regulatory catalyst — not a temporary PR event. The key distinction from normal regulatory risk is: (1) the evidence comes from the company's own internal research, making denial legally untenable, and (2) simultaneous multi-jurisdiction disclosure creates coordinated regulatory response that is harder to lobby away than single-country action. Trigger condition: named corporate employee provides internal research to 2+ government bodies in the same week AND technical distribution is present (MACD bearish + OBV falling + below SMA50) → open SHORT position at minimum allocation with thesis invalidation tied to regulatory inaction or credible structural company response within 60 days.

lessons: Lesson 41: When applying Lesson 31 (cover SHORT when MACD bullish) to a position that also shows OBV still falling, the Lesson 31 exit trigger does NOT fire — OBV direction is the override signal for institutional behavior. A SHORT with MACD bullish but OBV falling is experiencing a technical momentum bounce within ongoing institutional distribution; the distribution (OBV falling) is the dominant signal. Apply Lesson 38 analog to short positions: MACD bullish crossover during active OBV distribution is a false reversal. Cover trigger condition for shorts requires both MACD bullish AND OBV rising simultaneously per the original Lesson 31 specification.

lessons: Lesson 42: When a stock not currently held shows RSI<15 + ADX>50 + MACD bullish + OBV rising simultaneously — with no identifiable fundamental thesis invalidation — the Lesson 36 institutional accumulation framework supports opening a minimum-allocation new long position, not just holding an existing one. The extreme RSI reading in the context of a strong confirmed trend (ADX>50) with institutions buying (OBV rising) and momentum reversing (MACD bullish) is a statistically unusual divergence that typically precedes sharp reversals. Trigger condition for new entry: RSI<15 + ADX>50 + MACD bullish + OBV rising + no known fundamental thesis break → open minimum 5% position; do NOT add to size until price reclaims SMA50. The high ATR% during this period (often 6-9%+) confirms the volatility warrants minimum sizing. Invalidation: OBV turns falling OR MACD reverts bearish while still below SMA50.

lessons: Lesson 43: When 3 or more positions share a common commodity price driver (e.g., 4 energy positions: OXY, XOM, CVX, LNG all move with oil/gas prices), the portfolio's effective diversification is lower than the position count implies — a single OPEC decision or commodity price shock creates correlated drawdown across all names simultaneously. Treat the aggregate as a single position for risk concentration purposes. Trigger condition: 3+ positions sharing a primary commodity price driver AND at least one shows MACD bearish OR OBV flat → close the weakest technical setup to reduce correlated exposure to a maximum 2-position sector concentration. The position closed should be the one with the weakest OBV direction + worst MACD status combination, independent of whether the underlying commodity thesis remains intact at the portfolio level.

new_positions.thesis: Novo Nordisk is the dominant GLP-1 franchise globally — Ozempic and Victoza in diabetes, Wegovy launching into the obesity category, an entirely new multi-billion dollar indication with no credible competitor at scale. Type 2 diabetes affects 10%+ of the global population and GLP-1 agonists are displacing insulin as the new standard of care, driven by superior efficacy and cardiovascular outcome data. The obesity indication is a secular TAM expansion that consensus models have not yet fully priced: obesity affects ~650M adults globally and pharmacological treatment was historically inadequate. NVO's manufacturing moat (proprietary peptide synthesis at scale) and IP protection create a durable competitive advantage across a 5-10 year horizon. Zero direct COVID binary risk. Pure Aging Populations theme expression. RSI=72 is a clean non-overbought entry per Lesson 6 requirements. ADX=38 confirms a strong established trend. MACD bullish and OBV rising confirm institutional accumulation. This allocation is constrained to 4.5% to maintain the 20% cash floor after closing CRWD.

HD is not just technically superior — the thesis is structurally sound: home improvement spending is a durable beneficiary of the housing boom, work-from-home renovation cycle, aging housing stock, and Biden's infrastructure bill creating professional contractor demand flowing through HD's Pro segment (highest-margin, fastest-growing unit). Rising home equity across the country creates a sustained consumer wealth effect that HD captures directly. ADX=52 confirms an established institutional trend underway, not a momentum chase.

lessons: Lesson 44: In a broad market selloff during a low-news-flow period (e.g., holiday week with no major macro catalysts), do not treat MACD bearish alone as an exit trigger — nearly every stock will show MACD bearish simultaneously during positioning/risk-off events disconnected from individual fundamentals. The exit threshold during a broad selloff requires BOTH below SMA50 AND OBV falling together, not just MACD bearish. Positions that are MACD bearish but above SMA50 with OBV flat should be held — they are weathering the macro storm. Reserve exits for positions meeting the full distribution signal: below SMA50 + OBV falling. This prevents churning quality positions on market-structure noise rather than thesis-level deterioration.

lessons: Lesson 45: During a broad market selloff (Lesson 44 event: MACD bearish across 10+ positions simultaneously), the SHORT initiation threshold is asymmetrically favorable relative to normal market conditions. When a confirmed downtrend stock (ADX>35, below SMA50, OBV falling) shows continued distribution during a broad selloff, that stock is experiencing institutional acceleration of selling beyond the market-driven decline — institutions are choosing to sell it harder than the market is falling. This is a higher-confidence short entry, not lower. Trigger condition: existing downtrend candidate (RSI<40 + below SMA50 + ADX>35 + OBV falling) present during a broad market selloff (8+ positions MACD bearish simultaneously) → confidence in short entry INCREASES because OBV falling while the broad market is already selling confirms deliberate institutional distribution, not just passive market beta. Invalidation remains Lesson 31/41 combined trigger (MACD bullish AND OBV rising).

lessons: Lesson 46: When a regulatory body publishes a formal proposal (e.g., CPUC proposed decision, SEC proposed rule) that, if finalized, would materially reduce the economics of a portfolio company's primary growth driver cited in the thesis, treat the proposal itself — not the final rule — as the thesis risk event. Regulatory proposals in this category create sustained price overhang throughout the multi-month proceeding; the stock does not recover until resolution. Trigger condition: (1) named regulatory body publishes a formal proposal reducing a subsidy, incentive, or competitive advantage cited in the original thesis, AND (2) at least two technical distribution signals present (below SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV falling) → exit on the proposal announcement, not the final rule. The asymmetry is clear: if the proposal is rejected, the thesis accelerates and re-entry is available; if the proposal passes, exit at the proposal is far superior to exit at the final rule. Invalidation: proposal is formally withdrawn, rejected by the regulatory body, or materially amended in favor of the original thesis.

lessons: Lesson 47: When a portfolio company beats EPS AND beats revenue AND guides above consensus on both metrics in the same quarterly print — and the stock's OBV is simultaneously rising — this constitutes a maximum-conviction position addition signal even when MACD is bearish due to a broad low-volume selloff (Lesson 44 environment). The fundamental beat-and-raise combined with institutional accumulation (OBV rising) during a broad selloff is the clearest possible convergence of thesis confirmation and smart-money positioning. The broad market MACD bearish is structural noise; the company-specific OBV rising on an earnings catalyst is a deliberate institutional action. Trigger condition: EPS beat + revenue beat + guidance raise simultaneously AND OBV rising AND price above SMA50 → add to existing position toward maximum conviction allocation, subject to cash floor compliance. Distinct from normal earnings beats: all three outputs (EPS, revenue, guidance) must confirm in the same print.

new_positions.thesis: Eli Lilly is the highest-quality pharmaceutical compounder in the Aging Populations universe and the natural destination for capital freed by covering the T short. The tirzepatide obesity thesis is the most underappreciated catalyst in large-cap pharma: Phase 3 SURMOUNT data is approaching and positions LLY at the center of what could be the largest pharmaceutical category launch in history — roughly 100M Americans with clinical obesity potentially qualifying for GLP-1-based treatment at prices far above generic alternatives. The market has not priced this fully. Trulicity and Jardiance provide durable diabetes franchise cash flows that de-risk the pipeline optionality entirely. Donanemab (Alzheimer's) represents an additional unpriced call option on the largest unaddressed neurology market. As Fed rate hike expectations build for 2022, LLY's defensive earnings profile and low rate sensitivity make it structurally superior to our high-multiple tech positions during the rate normalization period. Unlike JNJ which carries pandemic exposure, LLY has zero direct COVID risk and pure aging demographics tailwind. Technical setup is one of the cleanest available: RSI=66 (healthy, non-overbought entry), above SMA50, MACD bullish, OBV rising confirming institutional accumulation ahead of catalysts, ADX=28 established trend formation. This fills the capital void from T short coverage with a confirmed long thesis that directly strengthens our Aging Populations theme.
new_positions.invalidation: OBV turns falling AND MACD turns bearish simultaneously while below SMA50 — full Lesson 22 distribution signal. Negative Phase 3 data readout on tirzepatide (SURMOUNT or SURPASS-6 cardiovascular outcome). FDA complete response letter on a key pipeline asset. Loss of SMA50 combined with two distribution signals.
new_positions.timing_note: RSI=66 is the ideal entry zone — above 50 confirms uptrend momentum, below 70 avoids overbought risk. OBV rising confirms institutional accumulation. Rate normalization environment structurally favors defensive healthcare compounders over high-multiple growth tech.

close_positions.reason: Lesson 31 exit trigger confirmed: SHORT position shows MACD bullish AND OBV rising simultaneously. Both conditions required by Lesson 31 for mandatory cover have fired. T is above SMA50 with ADX=27 showing institutional re-accumulation underway. The structural telecom commoditization thesis may be valid on a 2-year horizon but near-term timing is wrong — institutions are actively buying T, not selling it. Cover immediately per Lesson 31 — structural thesis correctness does not excuse holding a technically reversed short.

response.weekly_summary: Holiday week Dec 29: Minimal actionable news flow — cannabis lifestyle and crypto pieces dominate headlines, signaling nothing material. Single macro signal: building Fed rate hike expectations for 2022 confirmed by financial press. Primary portfolio action: covered T short per clean Lesson 31 trigger (MACD bullish + OBV rising in short position, both conditions required). Rotated freed capital into LLY at 6% — highest-quality Aging Populations entry with tirzepatide obesity catalyst as primary asymmetric driver. Portfolio returns to 15 positions with cash ~25%. Heading into Q1 2022, key watch items are: Fed rate hike timeline (headwind for MSFT/NVDA/AMD, tailwind for CVX/financials), UNH and MU both approaching RSI=75 Lesson 3 threshold, COST and MSFT OBV falling (Lesson 33 early-warning monitoring). Portfolio at +26.2% vs S&P +27.2% — gap is narrow. Quality positioning across AI, Healthcare, Energy, and Consumer themes should enable catch-up without taking on speculative risk.
